title: Gas Hydrate Estimate in an Area of Deformation and High Heat Flow at the Chile Triple Junction
abstract: Gas hydrate spatial distribution, concentration and geothermal gradients are estimated in the accretionary prism and forearc sediments offshore Taitao Peninsula (45.5°–47°S) near the Chile Triple Junction. Velocity analysis of seismic profile RC2901-751 indicates gas hydrate concentrations less than 10% of total rock volume and extremely high geothermal gradients. The large estimated methane volumes (7.21 × 10¹¹ m³ hydrate, 4.1 × 10¹⁰ m³ free gas) make this region a potential massive methane source.

location.description: Offshore Taitao Peninsula near Chile Triple Junction (45.5°–47°S); gas hydrate study on RC2901-751 line

data.name: R/V Conrad Line RC2901-751 — Taitao Peninsula / Chile Triple Junction (45.5°–47°S)
data.description: Named MCS line RC2901-751 from R/V Conrad offshore Taitao Peninsula near Chile Triple Junction; velocity analysis yields gas hydrate concentrations <10% total volume and extremely high geothermal gradients from BSR depth. [R/V Conrad RC2901 (Lamont, early 1990s) — no confirmed public data portal found]

_data_note: Open access MDPI paper. Line RC2901-751 from R/V Conrad 1990 cruise; pre-ODP Leg 141 site survey. Accessible via MGDS.

processing.summary: Velocity analysis was applied to the RC2901-751 MCS seismic line offshore Taitao Peninsula (Chile Triple Junction ~45.5°–47°S) to derive gas hydrate and free gas concentrations. PSDM yields velocity model for BSR characterization. Extremely high geothermal gradients are inferred from BSR depth in this high-heat-flow region associated with the Chile Ridge spreading center.
workflow: 4. Gas hydrate and free gas concentration estimation
5. Geothermal gradient calculation from BSR depth

analysis_notes: Open access MDPI Geosciences. RC2901-751 is a named line from R/V Conrad 1990 cruise, accessible via MGDS.
